workflows_introduction.description: No-Code allows you to manage and quantitatively analyze the individual steps of your processes and the associated data in a simple and user-friendly way.

process_flow_help.title: What is Process flow?
process_flow_help.description: A process flow represents the individual steps of your process and how they are connected. It's a visual representation of the sequence of actions or tasks that are part of a particular process. By mapping out these steps, you can clearly see how each part of the process leads to the next, identify any dependencies, and understand the overall 'No-Code'.
process_flow_help.requirements_description: Requirements refer to the data that must be entered or available to move an entry to the next step in the process. They are essential conditions or pieces of information needed at each stage to proceed. This could include specific inputs from users, approval from a manager, completion of a previous task, or any other necessary data point. Ensuring that these requirements are met is crucial for the smooth progression of the process from one step to the next.

node_types_description.title: Start Node
node_types_description.purpose: The entry point of the workflow, marking the beginning of the process.
node_types_description.usage: Every workflow must have exactly one Start Node.

node_types_description.title: Default Node
node_types_description.purpose: Represents the standard steps within the workflow.
node_types_description.usage: Allows connections from both the left and right sides to build the main structure of the workflow.

node_types_description.title: Terminal Node
node_types_description.purpose: Signifies the end of a process flow, marking the completion of a branch or the entire workflow.
node_types_description.usage: Multiple Terminal Nodes can be used to represent various endpoints.

node_types_description.title: Archive Node
node_types_description.purpose: Used to store or save data at a particular stage without progressing further.
node_types_description.usage: Archive Nodes do not allow incoming connections from the left side and are typically used for archiving purposes.

node_types_description.title: Node Group
node_types_description.usage: Node Groups allow you to group nodes together.

global_data_help.title: What is the collected data
global_data_help.description:
During the process of your 'No-Code' you usually want to collect data about the company, applicant, project or whatever your topic is.

Here you create a form that includes all data that you want to collect until the end of your 'No-Code'. We provide a lot of different types of fields as well as layouting and structuring options so that you can structure your data as intuitively as you want.

add_item.label: Label
add_item.enter_extension: Please enter file extension and press enter to add

global_data.allow_multiple_entries_tooltip: Enable this option to allow users to add multiple instances of this section within the project.
global_data.drag_items_to_section_text: Drag items to add fields into this section
global_data.page_delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete the entire page '{{page}}'?
global_data.page_delete_confirm_desc: This action will remove all associated data and cannot be undone.
global_data.section_delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete the entire section '{{section}}'?
global_data.section_delete_confirm_desc: This action will remove all associated data and cannot be undone.

integrations.project_planning_desc: Automatically link this project to our combined project and task management. A created work plan is automatically transferred and the task management allows a detailed effort and progress analysis. This allows you to plan efficiently and implement your projects successfully.
integrations.cost_planning_desc: If you want to plan or record other cost types in addition to personnel costs, such as contractors or other invoices for the project, our cost planning tool will help you.
integrations.task_management_desc: Automatically link the project to our Task Management. Any work plan created is automatically transferred there. Task management allows a detailed analysis of effort and progress.
integrations.cluster_space_desc: Automatically connect the project to our Cluster Space. Any work plan you have created is automatically transferred there. Cluster Space helps you to organize your folders and files.
integrations.pm_disabled_text: This integration is currently in use by an active project type and cannot be disabled

integrations_help.title: What is the collected data
integrations_help.description:
During the process of your 'No-Code' you usually want to collect data about the company, applicant, project or whatever your topic is.

Here you create a form that includes all data that you want to collect until the end of your 'No-Code'. We provide a lot of different types of fields as well as layouting and structuring options so that you can structure your data as intuitively as you want.

permissions_help.title: What are permissions
permissions_help.description:
Permissions allow you to control who sees which data and functionality and who can edit it

That way you can ensure that sensitive data can only be seen or edited by the correct person and that no one can fiddle with data they are not responsible for.
